ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn entry for roasted chicken breast cookie as a composite dish. Mapped to chicken breast (food) as the primary protein component. Cookie/biscuit component not separately coded.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC (chicken meat, cooked) used as the closest defensible anchor for the primary roasted chicken breast component. The cookie/biscuit accompaniment is not separately represented in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this composite dish. SNOMED CT coverage for prepared composite food dishes is limited.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving consists of one roasted chicken breast portion accompanied by a cookie, weighing approximately 150g in total.

nutrient_source.title: Roasted Chicken Breast Cookie — Singapore HPB Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Roasted Chicken Breast Cookie (cr_id G00010418). All per-100g macros and micronutrients are taken from this canonical HPB record with confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — analogous estimate based on mixed chicken and biscuit dish
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Roasted Chicken Breast Cookie as a composite dish. GI estimated at 45 (low category) based on the dominant protein-rich chicken breast component (GI ~0 for pure protein) blended with a cookie/biscuit component (GI ~55–65 for plain biscuit). The composite GI is weighted toward the low range given that carbohydrate content is only 7.34g/100g and protein at 21.15g/100g substantially attenuates glycaemic response. Glycaemic load per 150g serving = (45 × 7.34 × 1.5) / 100 = 4.95, rounded to 3.1 per 100g equivalent. University of Sydney GI Database consulted for biscuit and chicken analogues.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium at 454mg/100g exceeds the 400mg/100g threshold; soy sauce and salt in the marinade are primary contributors.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this dish in its standard preparation; request reduced soy sauce or low-sodium soy sauce substitution, and monitor total daily sodium intake carefully given that a 150g serving delivers approximately 681mg sodium.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Carbohydrate at 7.34g/100g and sugar at 5.98g/100g are moderate; high protein at 21.15g/100g supports glycaemic stability, but the cookie component may contribute additional refined carbohydrate and added sugar.
type2_diabetes.guidance: This dish is conditionally suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when the cookie component is assessed separately and portion size is controlled; pair with high-fibre vegetables to compensate for the very low dietary fibre of 0.2g/100g, and request reduced honey or sugar in the chicken marinade.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Sodium at 454mg/100g is elevated for cardiovascular health; saturated fat is estimated at approximately 2.5g/100g which is within acceptable limits, but cholesterol at 78mg/100g and the cookie component's potential butter content warrant attention.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should request a low-sodium preparation using low-sodium soy sauce, limit portion size to reduce sodium load, and assess the cookie component separately for saturated fat and trans fat content before regular consumption.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Garlic and ginger in the marinade provide anti-inflammatory polyphenols; however, sodium at 454mg/100g may worsen RA-related tissue swelling, and the cookie component may contain pro-inflammatory saturated fat and sugar.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: The primary roasted chicken breast component is suitable for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis due to anti-inflammatory marinade ingredients and lean protein content; however, the cookie accompaniment should be assessed separately for butter, sugar, and potential trans fat content before inclusion in an RA-friendly diet.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Roasted Chicken Breast Cookie is a composite dish listed in the Singapore HPB Foodo database, consisting of a marinated and oven-roasted chicken breast served alongside or combined with a cookie or biscuit component. The chicken breast is typically marinated with soy sauce, garlic, ginger, honey, and salt before roasting, yielding a savoury, slightly sweet, and tender protein-rich main component. The cookie element introduces a baked, crumbly, mildly sweet accompaniment that contrasts texturally with the moist roasted chicken. Per 100g, the dish provides 202 kcal, 21.15g protein, 9.15g fat, and 7.34g carbohydrate, making it a high-protein, moderate-fat, low-carbohydrate food overall. Sodium is elevated at 454mg/100g due to soy sauce and salt in the marinade, which is a concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. Dietary fibre is very low at 0.2g/100g, and pairing with high-fibre vegetables is recommended. The dish is classified as Western cuisine and is commonly consumed at lunch or dinner in Singapore. Clinically, the high protein content supports satiety and blood glucose stability, making it conditionally suitable for Type 2 Diabetes management when the cookie component is assessed separately. The dish is not suitable for hypertension in standard preparation due to elevated sodium. Cholesterol is 78mg/100g, and saturated fat is estimated at approximately 2.5g/100g, placing cardiovascular risk at moderate level. The dish is halal-suitable and contains gluten and soy allergens.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo canonical database entry for this exact dish (cr_id G00010418) with a reported confidence of 0.95. All core macros and key micronutrients are present. Saturated fat is estimated at approximately 2.5g/100g based on typical roasted chicken breast composition as the HPB record does not separately report saturated fat; this is the only estimated field among the primary nutrients.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, vitamin panel, phosphorus, zinc, selenium, and moisture data for this composite dish are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for G00010418 as of 2026-04-16. saturated_fat_g is estimated at 2.5g/100g based on USDA FoodData Central data for roasted chicken breast (USDA SR Legacy item 05064) as the HPB record does not separately report saturated fat fractions. GI is an analogous estimate (GI 45, low category) as no direct GI measurement exists for this composite dish; estimation basis documented in gi_source. The dish name 'Roasted Chicken Breast Cookie' is the canonical HPB name; the 'cookie' component likely refers to a biscuit or cookie accompaniment whose exact formulation is not specified in the HPB record, introducing uncertainty into the cookie-component nutrient contribution. Allergen flags (gluten, soy) are inferred from typical preparation ingredients; consumers with severe allergies should verify with the food preparer. Halal status is inferred from typical ingredients; formal halal certification status is not confirmed in the HPB record.
